title: Han Solo's pirate ship
content:
{{Top|ncl}}
{{Citation}}
{{Cut|canon=1|Star Wars drafts}}
{{Conjecture|noncanon}}
'''Han's [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] ship''' was an unnamed [[TDF104]] formerly owned by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Oxus]], a pirate and [[Spice/Legends|spice]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]]. After running up a huge debt with Oxus following a botched spice run, Han stole the ship to give [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[R2-D2/Legends|Artoo]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|Threepio]] a ride to [[Ogana Major]]. His friends, [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross Holdaack]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], helped him fake a reactor overload, causing the crew loyal to Oxus to evacuate when it was docked at [[Docking Port 23]] in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]].

The ship was modified with secret compartments. The extent of other changes from the base model was unknown, though it was a custom construction.

When a [[Unknown Sith Knight (Alderaan Docking Bay)|Sith Knight]] examined the ship, captured at [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], he referred to it as "drift junk". Holdaack also expressed a belief that going into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyper-skip]] might cause it to fall apart.

When Han had a change of heart and entered the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the pirate ship was critically damaged after [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] executed a kamikaze run on it. The crew evacuated in a [[Escape pod/Legends|life pod]] and the ship was destroyed with the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]].

==Behind the scenes==
The ship was called the "Pirate ship" in the draft scripts and during visual development of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Episode IV]], acquiring the name [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] later.

The ship was the conceptual forerunner of the [[Tantive IV/Legends|Tantive IV]], as Han's ship was changed to the now familiar disc design after the ship was found to be too similar to a ship featured on ''Space: 1999''.
